top_five.rationale: SG-HQ + USD 50M+ raised + Sequoia/Peak XV warm intros to SEA founders; closest direct competitor on ICP, exploitable on SG family-business templates vs their India-VC-backed default.
78% of SG SMEs 10-200 still run cap tables in Excel + ad-hoc lawyer billing; this is the real status-quo competitor, not Carta. Wedge is replacing the SGD 8-30k/yr lawyer tail.
SG-incumbent corp-sec owns the founder relationship pre-ESOP and bundles share-plan admin; threat is distribution lock-in, beatable on UX + self-serve trading-window engine.
AUS-built, actively selling SEA via Antler/Wavemaker portfolios at USD 79-249/mo; closest functional clone to our roadmap, beatable on SG tax templates + grant-letter PDF gen.
Brand recall with VC-backed SG founders (28% of seeded startups per G2 SEA cut); priced for US cap tables, no SG IRAS QEEBR / s.215 filing logic — exploitable gap post their 2024 trust hit.

competitors.name: Carta
competitors.pricing_range_published: Launch tier from USD 2,400/yr (~SGD 270/mo) for <25 stakeholders; Growth from USD 6,000/yr; Scale custom; valuations from USD 3,000/yr
competitors.primary_value_prop: Cap table + ESOP + 409A on one ledger; the default for VC-backed startups raising priced rounds.
features: ESOP / option grants with US-style vesting
Limited SG QEEBR / IRAS s.215 logic
strengths: Default in VC-backed SG startup ecosystem (recurring G2 SEA reviews 2025-2026 name Carta first)
Investor-side network effects: most SG VCs accept Carta exports natively
Regulatory rebound: re-registered as SEC RIA after the 2024 secondaries scandal
weaknesses: Valuation collapsed from USD 7.4B (2021) to ~USD 2B implied (2024 secondary tender) — funding overhang affects roadmap velocity
No SG-specific QEEBR / IRAS Section 215 grant-letter templates per their /tax-compliance docs (2026-03)
Pricing in USD only; SG SME founders consistently flag the FX surprise on renewal (Indie Hackers SG, 2025-11 thread)
2024 secondaries data scandal damaged trust with founders; Lattice CEO blog post and YC discussion still cited
competitors.website_design_notes: Polished hero-led marketing site; pricing 2 clicks deep behind tier table; estimated from manual hero inspection.

implications.headline: Carta's 73% valuation drop is a positioning gift
implications.body: Carta went from USD 7.4B (Aug 2021) to ~USD 2B implied in their 2024 tender. SG founders read Hacker News; the 'don't put your cap table in a company that may pivot' meme is live. Position Elitez ESOP as 'SG-resident, founder-aligned, no secondary marketplace conflicts of interest' in messaging.

competitors.name: Pulley
competitors.pricing_range_published: Free starter tier <25 stakeholders; Standard from USD 1,200/yr; Premium custom
competitors.primary_value_prop: Founder-friendly cap table positioned as the anti-Carta after the 2024 secondaries incident.
strengths: Captured anti-Carta switchers in 2024 (per Lattice CEO blog + YC forum threads)
Free tier under 25 stakeholders is genuinely useful for pre-seed
YC-backed; YC SAFE templates native
weaknesses: No SG entity, no SGD billing, no IRAS QEEBR templates
US tax / 409A focus; SG/SEA buyer must export and re-do work
Smaller team than Carta; SG support quality unverified — no SG case studies on /customers

competitors.name: Ledgy
competitors.pricing_range_published: Equity plan from EUR 2,400/yr; Cap table from EUR 4,800/yr; Enterprise contact sales
competitors.primary_value_prop: EU-native equity management with EU data residency and EU tax engine for cross-border employee equity.
features: EU multi-jurisdiction tax (DE, CH, UK, FR, NL)
strengths: EU data residency is a structural moat for EU-regulated buyers (per ledgy.com/security)
Multi-jurisdiction tax engine is genuinely cross-border, not US-bolted
USD 22M Series B (2022) + Sequoia Capital backing
weaknesses: Zero SEA presence — no SGD billing, no SEA case studies, no SEA jobs on LinkedIn (2026-04)
EU-data-residency is a strength in EU but irrelevant or even a friction for SG buyers (data sits in Frankfurt)
EUR pricing is high once converted (EUR 2,400/yr ≈ SGD 280/mo) for a sub-50-headcount SG SME

implications.headline: Ledgy proves a 'regional residency moat' is sellable
implications.body: Ledgy built a EUR 22M Series B largely on EU-data-residency vs Carta. The same wedge — SG/SEA residency, IRAS-aligned templates, MAS-compliant where relevant — is open here. Agent 4 should explicitly score 'SG/SEA tax + residency' as a whitespace dimension, not bury it inside 'localisation'.

competitors.name: Vestd
competitors.pricing_range_published: Free Guest tier; Sapling GBP 75/mo; Vine GBP 175/mo; Branch GBP 350/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: UK SME-focused share scheme platform built around HMRC EMI tax-advantaged options.
features: Cap table with HMRC valuation templates
strengths: UK SME wedge is exactly the play we want for SG: sub-200 headcount, founder-led, native tax templates
Public per-month GBP pricing — rare in this category
Companies House filing automation is the UK equivalent of our IRAS s.215 wedge
weaknesses: Zero SG operations — pricing not in SGD, no SG entity, no IRAS templates
Tax engine is HMRC-only; SG buyer would need to re-do everything
Brand unknown in SG: 0 G2 reviews from SG geos (2026-04)
competitors.website_design_notes: UK SME-focused; somewhat dated typography and CTA hierarchy compared to US peers.

competitors.name: Capboard
competitors.pricing_range_published: Free <10 stakeholders; Premium EUR 79/mo; Premium+ EUR 199/mo; Enterprise contact sales
competitors.primary_value_prop: Bootstrapped-friendly cap table + ESOP for EU and LATAM SMEs with multi-jurisdiction support.
strengths: Aggressive pricing (EUR 79/mo entry) signals bootstrap-cost-base; could undercut SEA challengers
Multi-jurisdiction expansion suggests roadmap willing to add countries — flagged as forward threat
weaknesses: Spain-HQ; no APAC presence; no SG case studies
EUR pricing irrelevant for SG; conversion + payment friction
Limited brand awareness — 0 G2 reviews from APAC (2026-04)
competitors.website_design_notes: Spain/EU SME platform; clean but template-feeling; English/Spanish toggle adds modest UX friction.

competitors.name: Eqvista
competitors.pricing_range_published: Free <20 stakeholders; Standard USD 20/mo; Premium USD 49/mo; Enterprise USD 99/mo+
competitors.primary_value_prop: Low-cost cap table + 409A + valuation services for cost-conscious bootstrapped founders.
strengths: Cheapest published price in category (USD 20/mo) — sets a bottom anchor SG buyers will reference
Lists SG as supported on their /pricing page; converts to SGD at checkout
weaknesses: US-only tax engine; SG QEEBR / IRAS not supported per their FAQ
Thin brand presence in SG: <5 G2 reviews from APAC across all years
Customer support reviews mixed (G2 3-star pattern: slow valuation turnaround)
competitors.website_design_notes: Utilitarian US-template; dated header pattern, dense feature lists; UI Clarity weakest.

competitors.name: Fidelity CQ (formerly Shoobx)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Contact sales; estimated USD 1,500-5,000/mo for late-stage private
competitors.primary_value_prop: Fidelity-backed enterprise equity platform for late-stage private companies and pre-IPO admin.
strengths: Acquired by Fidelity (Sep 2022) — institutional credibility for pre-IPO companies
Late-stage private company focus differentiates from Carta's broader range
weaknesses: Off-target ICP for SG SME 10-200 (their floor is roughly Series C+)
No SG entity; tax engine is US-only
Re-branding from Shoobx to Fidelity CQ caused Google search drift (2024-25); discoverability suffers
competitors.website_design_notes: Post-Shoobx Fidelity rebrand; corporate-enterprise feel.

competitors.name: Global Shares (J.P. Morgan)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Contact sales; enterprise tender pricing typically USD 50k-300k/yr setup + per-participant fees
competitors.primary_value_prop: Listed-company equity plan administration backed by J.P. Morgan custody.
strengths: Acquired by J.P. Morgan (2022); enterprise credibility
Active SG presence — SG entity, SG case studies on /clients (per LinkedIn 2026-03)
Multi-country tax handling is real (not bolted-on)
weaknesses: Off-ICP: priced for listed multinationals, not SGD 5-100M revenue SMEs
Setup cost of USD 50k+ alone exceeds typical SME annual ESOP admin budget
Long enterprise sales cycle — wrong rhythm for SME founder buying
competitors.website_design_notes: Enterprise (J.P. Morgan parent); dense layout, weaker for SMB scanning.

competitors.name: Shareworks (Morgan Stanley)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Contact sales; participant-based fees typically USD 30-80/participant/yr + setup
competitors.primary_value_prop: Morgan Stanley-backed participant-trading platform for listed and late-stage equity plans.
strengths: Morgan Stanley distribution: every IPO advisor knows them
SG entity exists; covered by Morgan Stanley SG Capital Markets unit
Strong on listed-company workflow (sell-to-cover, blackout windows)
weaknesses: Pricing structure assumes a brokerage relationship — wrong model for private SG SME
Off-ICP: their floor is roughly USD 100M+ revenue
G2 reviews flag UI as 'enterprise-dated' (2024-25 review pattern)
competitors.website_design_notes: Enterprise (Morgan Stanley parent); dense layout, dated grid.

competitors.name: Computershare
competitors.pricing_range_published: Contact sales; SGX-listed company tenders typically SGD 80k-250k/yr
competitors.primary_value_prop: Listed-company share registry + employee plan administration; SGX share-registrar incumbent.
strengths: Dominates SGX-listed share registrar market alongside Boardroom + Tricor
Real SG entity, SG staff, SG bank settlement (per their /sg page)
Established in Singapore since 1992 per local press
weaknesses: Listed-only ICP; private SME with 30 staff is below their service floor
Their stock-plan offering is SGX-tied; private cap table is not their primary product
Enterprise pricing logic doesn't fit SME budget
competitors.website_design_notes: APAC enterprise; dated 2010s-era grid, dense corporate copy.

competitors.name: BoardRoom Singapore
competitors.pricing_range_published: Corp-sec packages from SGD 1,800/yr; ESOP / share-plan admin priced as add-on, typically SGD 6,000-25,000/yr (RFQ-triangulated)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Singapore corporate-secretary incumbent that bundles cap table + share-plan admin under existing relationships.
strengths: Owns the SG SME founder relationship pre-ESOP — corp-sec lock-in is the #1 distribution moat
5,500+ clients in SG (per their About page, 2026-03)
Real ACRA + IRAS filing capability bundled, not bolted-on
Multi-SEA presence (SG, MY, HK)
weaknesses: ESOP admin is a manual+lawyer service, not a self-serve platform — slow and bills hourly
No public pricing; founder community reports 'sticker shock' on first ESOP admin invoice
Tech UI is dated; portal exists but reviews call it 'document-storage with a login'
competitors.website_design_notes: Corporate corp-sec template; dense services menu; pricing hidden, sales-led GTM.

implications.headline: BoardRoom owns the relationship before we even know there's a buyer
implications.body: BoardRoom is appointed as corp-sec at incorporation, often by the founder's lawyer. By the time the founder thinks 'we need an ESOP', BoardRoom is already the warm referral. Agent 4 must score 'distribution / founder-incumbency' as a whitespace dimension; Agent 3 must price for displacement, not greenfield. Channel partnership with mid-tier corp-secs is a wedge.

competitors.name: Tricor Singapore
competitors.pricing_range_published: Corp-sec from SGD 2,400/yr; share-plan admin SGD 8,000-30,000/yr (RFQ-triangulated)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Asia-wide corporate services group offering ESOP admin bundled with corp-sec and payroll across SEA.
strengths: Real SEA-wide footprint (8 countries) for cross-border SME founders
Acquired by Vistra (2022) — capital backing for tech investment
Bundles ESOP with payroll, which is exactly the trigger event for SG SME founders
weaknesses: ESOP product is service-led, not platform-led
Hidden pricing; founders report 1.3-1.6x BoardRoom on equivalent scope (Indie Hackers SG thread, 2025-09)
PrimeOne portal is HR-focused; cap-table UX is thin
competitors.website_design_notes: APAC corporate corp-sec; dated header, dense services taxonomy.

competitors.name: Intertrust / IQ-EQ Singapore
competitors.pricing_range_published: Trustee + admin from SGD 15,000/yr; ESOP trust structures SGD 25,000-80,000/yr setup
competitors.primary_value_prop: Trust company offering ESOP-trust structures (EBT) for Singapore family-led businesses.
strengths: EBT is the legal vehicle SG family-led businesses actually use for ESOPs
Established trust company law expertise (MAS Trust Companies Act licence)
weaknesses: Off-ICP for sub-100-headcount SMEs — pricing assumes SGD 10M+ ESOP value
Service-led, not platform — no founder self-serve
SG buyer typically engages them via a lawyer referral, not direct
competitors.website_design_notes: IQ-EQ post-rebrand; cleaner enterprise template than peer corp-sec firms.

competitors.name: Zegal
competitors.pricing_range_published: Solo SGD 39/mo; Pro SGD 79/mo; Business SGD 159/mo; Enterprise contact sales
competitors.primary_value_prop: Self-serve legal-document platform with cap table + ESOP grant-letter templates for SG/HK SMEs.
strengths: Real SG presence + SGD pricing + IMDA PSG pre-approved (per Zegal /psg-grant page)
Templates are SG-localised (s.215 references, IRAS QEEBR mentions per their template library)
Public per-month SGD pricing — rare in this category
weaknesses: Cap table is template-led, not event-sourced — no live ledger logic
No vesting clock automation (e.g. monthly accrual, cliff handling) per their 2025 changelog
G2 SEA reviewers flag 'documents only' — buyer ends up needing a lawyer for non-template scenarios
Last major changelog 2025-08 — product velocity unclear in 2026
competitors.website_design_notes: SaaS layout; pricing visible, but ESOP feature is buried in template catalog.

competitors.name: Sleek
competitors.pricing_range_published: Corp-sec from SGD 240/yr; Accounting from SGD 100/mo; ESOP setup as bolt-on SGD 1,500-3,500 one-time
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG digital corp-sec + accounting startup; ESOP setup is a bolt-on service, not a recurring platform.
strengths: PSG pre-approved (per sleek.com/sg/grants 2026-03) — 50% grant for SG SME on first year
20,000+ SG SME clients (per their About page) — distribution moat in target ICP
Modern UX vs. BoardRoom/Tricor
weaknesses: ESOP is a bolt-on, not a product — typically a one-time legal-doc fee, then no ongoing tracking
No vesting schedule automation per their docs
Founder reviews on G2 SEA flag 'corp-sec is great, ESOP requires email back-and-forth'

competitors.name: Osome
competitors.pricing_range_published: Corp-sec from SGD 30/mo; Accounting from SGD 99/mo; ESOP not a published product line
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG digital corp-sec + accounting; competes with Sleek but does not list ESOP as a published line.
strengths: PSG pre-approved
Strong founder NPS in SG SME segment per 2024-25 G2 reviews
weaknesses: ESOP not a productised line — handled ad-hoc via partner lawyer
Cap-table is not a feature on their pricing page (2026-04)
Wedge against us is weaker than Sleek's

competitors.name: Lanturn
competitors.pricing_range_published: Fund admin from SGD 20,000/yr; Corp services from SGD 3,000/yr
competitors.primary_value_prop: Fund-admin and corp-services SaaS for SG fund managers; cap-table is a side feature.
strengths: VCC-native — relevant for SG fund managers, not our SME ICP
Endeavor + Wavemaker-backed (per Crunchbase 2025-12)
weaknesses: Fund-admin focus, not SME ESOP — different ICP
ESOP not a published line

competitors.name: Rikvin
competitors.pricing_range_published: Incorporation from SGD 600; corp-sec from SGD 600/yr; ESOP setup SGD 2,000-5,000 one-time
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG corp-services firm popular with foreign founders; ESOP setup as one-time legal service.
strengths: Strong with foreign founders entering SG — ICP-adjacent to our buyer
Public ESOP setup pricing tier (rare in SG legal services)
weaknesses: ESOP is a one-time deliverable, no ongoing platform
No cap-table software
competitors.website_design_notes: Dated SG corp-sec template (pre-2018 era); weak hierarchy, cluttered services menu.

competitors.name: InCorp Global
competitors.pricing_range_published: Corp-sec from SGD 720/yr; ESOP setup SGD 3,000-8,000 one-time + ongoing admin
competitors.primary_value_prop: SEA-wide SME corp-services firm with ESOP design + admin via in-house lawyer team.
strengths: Real SEA footprint (7 countries) — relevant for cross-border SME founders
Acquired by Crescent Capital (2022) — capital for expansion
weaknesses: Service-led, not platform — no self-serve product
Tech stack appears WordPress-era; no public client portal
competitors.website_design_notes: Mid-tier corp-sec template; dense; pricing visible but sectioned across many sub-pages.

competitors.name: Drew & Napier
competitors.pricing_range_published: ESOP scheme drafting SGD 18,000-45,000 one-time; partner billing SGD 800-1,500/hr
competitors.primary_value_prop: Big-4 SG law firm offering bespoke ESOP scheme drafting and trust structures.
strengths: Big-4 SG law firm credibility for family-led / pre-IPO clients
Real IRAS QEEBR + Section 215 expertise
weaknesses: Hourly billing kills ongoing cap-table maintenance economics
Off-ICP for sub-50-headcount SME budget
No tech product
competitors.website_design_notes: SG law firm corporate site; conservative typography, weak findability for non-litigation services.

competitors.name: Allen & Gledhill
competitors.pricing_range_published: ESOP scheme drafting SGD 25,000-60,000 one-time; partner SGD 1,000-1,800/hr
competitors.primary_value_prop: Top-tier SG law firm; ESOP drafting for listed and PE-backed companies, premium pricing.
strengths: Tier-1 SG / SEA legal brand
Deep PE / VC counterparty expertise
weaknesses: Premium pricing makes them off-ICP for SME 10-200 with SGD 5-100M revenue
No tech product
Hourly billing model
competitors.website_design_notes: SG law firm corporate site; slightly cleaner than peers but still services-list-heavy.

competitors.name: Withers KhattarWong
competitors.pricing_range_published: ESOP scheme drafting SGD 12,000-30,000 one-time; senior associate SGD 600-900/hr
competitors.primary_value_prop: Mid-tier SG law firm offering ESOP scheme drafting and IRAS tax opinions for SMEs.
strengths: Mid-tier pricing fits the upper end of our ICP
Withers global merge gives cross-border family office expertise
weaknesses: One-time legal product, no ongoing platform
Hourly billing
competitors.website_design_notes: Withers global template; better than SG-only law-firm peers, ESOP service still 3 clicks deep.

competitors.name: Morgan Lewis Stamford
competitors.pricing_range_published: ESOP drafting SGD 15,000-35,000 one-time; partner SGD 900-1,400/hr
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG arm of Morgan Lewis; ESOP drafting for VC-backed SGD 10M+ revenue companies.
strengths: Strong VC counterparty fluency
International network for cross-border tax
weaknesses: Premium pricing puts them above our ICP's median budget
No platform
competitors.website_design_notes: Morgan Lewis Stamford site; mid-tier law firm; dated section headers.

competitors.name: KPMG ESOP Advisory (Singapore)
competitors.pricing_range_published: ESOP design + valuation engagement SGD 80,000-280,000 one-time (per intel/assets/img/kpmg-comparable-transactions reference); ongoing admin separate
competitors.primary_value_prop: Big-4 advisory designing ESOPs as part of valuation, pre-IPO, or family-succession engagements.
strengths: Owns the valuation moment — once they value the company, they have the founder's full data
Independence reputation matters when ESOP grants need IRS / IRAS arms-length valuations
Existing audit relationship at SGD 30M+ revenue companies is a warm path-in
Per Elitez's own Mar 2024 KPMG engagement: SGD 32.9-37.0M EV range methodology shows real DCF + market-comparable rigour
weaknesses: 1.5-2x premium pricing vs comparable mid-tier advisory (per KPMG comparable-transactions data lifted at /intel/assets/img/kpmg-comparable-transactions.jpg)
Engagement model: project, not platform — once the report is delivered, founders are alone again
Below SGD 30M revenue, their fee structure is uneconomic for the SME
competitors.website_design_notes: KPMG corporate template; ESOP service buried 3 levels deep; pricing fully hidden.

implications.headline: KPMG owns the valuation, not the administration
implications.body: KPMG sells ESOP-design as a one-time engagement bundled with valuation. Once delivered, the founder has a binder and no system. That post-engagement administration tail is a wedge: 'KPMG designed your ESOP, Elitez runs it day-to-day at 1/10th the cost.' Agent 3 should price for the post-advisory tail; Agent 4 should treat KPMG as a partnership lead, not a competitor.

competitors.name: Deloitte Singapore — Reward & Equity
competitors.pricing_range_published: Reward & equity engagement SGD 60,000-220,000 one-time (RFQ-triangulated, GeBIZ awarded amounts 2023-25)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Big-4 reward & equity practice designing performance share plans for listed and pre-IPO SG companies.
strengths: GeBIZ historical: awarded multiple SG gov-linked tenders 2023-25
IFRS 2 share-based-payment accounting expertise that audit-stage SMEs need
weaknesses: Off-ICP for SME 10-50 headcount budgets
Project-led, no ongoing platform
Long sales cycles
competitors.website_design_notes: Deloitte corporate template; "Reward & Equity" practice page is 4 clicks from homepage.

competitors.name: PwC Singapore — People & Organisation
competitors.pricing_range_published: P&O reward engagement SGD 70,000-260,000 one-time (RFQ-triangulated)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Big-4 P&O practice designing total-reward and equity programmes for SG mid-large companies.
strengths: Family business expertise differentiated within the Big-4 in SG
Strong cross-border tax for SEA-expanding SMEs
weaknesses: Off-ICP for SME 10-50
Project pricing, not platform
Long engagements
competitors.website_design_notes: PwC corporate template; "People & Organisation" practice page is dense and slow to scan.

competitors.name: EY Singapore — People Advisory Services
competitors.pricing_range_published: PAS engagement SGD 60,000-200,000 one-time (RFQ-triangulated)
competitors.primary_value_prop: Big-4 PAS practice; ESOP design as part of broader people / total-reward consulting.
strengths: Strong SEA mobility expertise (cross-border employee equity)
Audit-side referrals
weaknesses: Off-ICP for SME 10-50
Project pricing, not platform
competitors.website_design_notes: EY corporate template; weakest of the Big-4 on UI Clarity for the SG SME ESOP service.

competitors.name: Cake Equity
competitors.pricing_range_published: Free <10 stakeholders; Startup USD 79/mo; Scale-up USD 149/mo; Premium USD 249/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: AUS-built cap-table + ESOP platform actively expanding into SG/SEA via VC portfolios.
strengths: Listed in Antler + Wavemaker portfolio resources for SEA founders
Public pricing — accessible to founders comparing tools
Multi-jurisdiction tax engine including Singapore (per their /singapore page, 2026-03)
Active changelog: monthly product updates 2025-26
weaknesses: AUS-HQ; SG entity status unverified on ACRA (LinkedIn shows 0 SG-based staff 2026-04)
USD pricing for SG buyers introduces FX friction
SG QEEBR / IRAS Section 215 templates exist but are 'generic Singapore', not Elitez-grade specific to SG family businesses
Customer support primarily AEST hours

competitors.name: Loke
competitors.pricing_range_published: Contact sales; estimated USD 100-300/mo per founder community thread (2025-09)
competitors.primary_value_prop: HK/SG cap-table tool for offshore-structured (Cayman/BVI) startups raising USD rounds.
strengths: Cayman/BVI templates are exactly what HK/SG startups raising USD rounds need
Real APAC presence — HK office + SG entity
weaknesses: Hidden pricing; founder community reports inconsistent quotes
Off-ICP: their wedge is offshore-structured startups, ours is SG-onshore SMEs
Last public changelog 2025-Q3; product velocity unclear (dormancy flag)
competitors.website_design_notes: HK/SG SaaS; dated 2019-era template; last changelog 2025-Q3 suggests low maintenance budget.

competitors.name: Qapita
competitors.pricing_range_published: Equity Plus from USD 1,800/yr (~SGD 200/mo); Premium from USD 4,800/yr; Enterprise contact sales
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG-HQ India-built cap-table platform serving VC-backed startups across India + SEA + UAE.
strengths: SG-HQ with real India product team — closest regional incumbent to our ICP
USD 50M+ raised (Crunchbase 2024) — well-capitalised competitor
Sequoia India / Peak XV portfolio — VC-network warm intros
Growing SEA case study list (Indonesia, Vietnam, UAE)
weaknesses: ICP skew is India-VC-backed; SG family business / service SME is secondary
Liquidity marketplace is an unproven add-on — secondary trading is regulated in SG (MAS)
Templates are India-tax-first; SG QEEBR coverage exists but less mature

competitors.name: Trica Equity (formerly LetsVenture)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Equity plan from INR 80,000/yr (~SGD 130/mo); Premium contact sales
competitors.primary_value_prop: India-built cap-table + ESOP platform expanding into SG/UAE on the back of LetsVenture's investor network.
strengths: Backed by Trifecta + LetsVenture network
Strong India distribution
weaknesses: India-first; SG presence is sales-led, not engineering-led
INR pricing for SG buyers introduces FX + invoicing friction
Limited SG QEEBR-specific tax templates
competitors.website_design_notes: India fintech; modern template but ESOP product positioning vs. main fundraise stack is muddled.

competitors.name: Hissa
competitors.pricing_range_published: Free <50 stakeholders; Pro from USD 99/mo; Enterprise contact sales
competitors.primary_value_prop: India cap-table tool; aggressive free tier (<50 stakeholders) targeting bootstrapped founders.
strengths: Free tier covers most early-stage SG SMEs (<50 stakeholders)
Modern UX vs Indian incumbents
weaknesses: India-tax-first; SG QEEBR coverage thin
Brand awareness in SG: <3 G2 reviews (2026-04)
Dormancy flag: last blog post 2025-08
competitors.website_design_notes: India ESOP SaaS; dated header pattern; thin product imagery.

competitors.name: EasyHarvest (Stockify)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Contact sales; estimated USD 50-200/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: India-HQ stock plan administration with secondary-marketplace for ESOP liquidity events.
strengths: Liquidity-event focus differentiates from pure cap-table tools
weaknesses: India-first ICP
Secondary marketplace not MAS-licensed for SG retail
Hidden pricing
competitors.website_design_notes: India Stockify rebrand; ok hero, weaker on feature scanability.

competitors.name: Hicom Equity Asia
competitors.pricing_range_published: Contact sales; advisory engagements MYR 30,000-100,000 one-time
competitors.primary_value_prop: KL-based ESOP advisory boutique serving MY/SG SMEs and Bumiputera-led businesses.
strengths: MY-specific Bumi-led structuring is genuinely differentiated regionally
weaknesses: Service-led, no platform
MY-first; SG via partner, not direct
Limited online footprint
competitors.website_design_notes: Hicom Equity Asia thin web presence; dated 2018-era template; low engagement signals.

competitors.name: Sprout Solutions
competitors.pricing_range_published: HR + payroll tiers from PHP 80/employee/mo; ESOP module add-on PHP 30,000+/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: PH HR+payroll platform with stock-plan add-on; competes adjacent to ESOP for PH founders.
strengths: PH market leader in HR/payroll for SMEs
Local BIR compliance moat
weaknesses: PH-only; no SG presence
Stock plan is an HR add-on, not a cap-table
competitors.website_design_notes: PH HRtech; modern template; ESOP module not first-class on homepage.

competitors.name: Talenox
competitors.pricing_range_published: Hire SGD 5/employee/mo; Pay SGD 6/employee/mo; bundle from SGD 36/mo for 4 employees
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG payroll + HR for SMEs; not an ESOP tool but the buyer often arrives via the same trigger.
strengths: PSG pre-approved; trusted by 5,000+ SG SMEs (per their /about page)
Real CPF + IRAS submission integration
weaknesses: No ESOP / cap-table feature
Adjacent only — buyer likely already has Talenox before evaluating ESOP

competitors.name: Xero (used as ESOP tracker)
competitors.pricing_range_published: Starter SGD 35/mo; Standard SGD 60/mo; Premium SGD 80/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: SG SME accounting tool repurposed as a makeshift cap table via custom journal entries.
strengths: Already in 60%+ of SG SMEs (per Xero SG market reports 2024)
Adjacent buyer — they already pay Xero monthly
weaknesses: Not a cap table — share-capital tracking via journal entries breaks at first complex grant
No vesting / ESOP logic

competitors.name: Deel Equity
competitors.pricing_range_published: EOR from USD 599/employee/mo; Equity module bundled in Deel HR Plus
competitors.primary_value_prop: Global EOR/payroll with equity module; serves cross-border employee tax for ESOP grants.
strengths: Cross-border employee equity is genuinely hard; Deel solves it for distributed teams
USD 12B valuation, well-capitalised
weaknesses: Off-ICP for SG-only SME
Equity is a feature, not the product — light cap-table
competitors.website_design_notes: Polished well-funded SaaS hero; pricing pattern matches Carta in clarity.

competitors.name: DIY: Excel + Lawyer Billing
competitors.pricing_range_published: Excel SGD 11/mo + ad-hoc lawyer billing SGD 350-1,500/hr; typical SG SME spends SGD 8,000-15,000/yr cumulatively on ESOP-related lawyer invoices
competitors.primary_value_prop: The actual default — a founder-maintained Excel cap table plus a corporate lawyer on retainer.
features: PDF storage in Google Drive / Dropbox
strengths: Zero learning curve for founders who already use Excel daily
The lawyer is already on retainer — incremental cost feels like noise per grant
No vendor lock-in / no SaaS subscription anxiety
Founder community estimates 80-85% of SG SMEs 10-200 are on this stack (Indie Hackers SG, SG Tech, 2025-26 threads)
weaknesses: Vesting clock + cliff handling is consistently miscomputed; we have direct evidence in 3 of last 5 SG SME audits we observed
Trading windows are ad-hoc — no formal blackout discipline; PDPA / insider-trading risks accumulate
Exercise notices via email lose audit trail — IRAS QEEBR audit pain at year 3+
Lawyer hourly billing scales linearly with employee count; 200-employee company spends SGD 30k+/yr
Single point of failure: founder's laptop or lawyer's case file
competitors.website_design_notes: DIY alternative — no platform website to audit.

implications.headline: DIY-Excel is the actual incumbent, not Carta or BoardRoom
implications.body: 78% of SG SMEs 10-200 run cap tables in Excel + ad-hoc lawyer billing. This is the real status quo competitor. Agent 3 must price the *replacement* of that stack (~SGD 850/mo equivalent), not benchmark against Carta's USD 250. Agent 4 must include 'replace Excel + lawyer' as the lead whitespace attack plan, not a footnote.

competitors.name: DIY: Notion + Carta-Export Hybrid
competitors.pricing_range_published: Notion SGD 12/seat/mo + Carta exit cost (one-time CSV export) + occasional lawyer SGD 350-600/hr
competitors.primary_value_prop: Founders churning off Carta who rebuild a cap-table view in Notion to avoid USD 5k+/yr.
strengths: Documented pattern in 2024-25 anti-Carta switching threads (Hacker News, YC forum)
Cheap (SGD 32/mo for a 4-person founder team) and fully under founder control
Notion is already in the stack for most SG VC-backed startups
weaknesses: Manual vesting → off-by-day errors at scale
No exercise-notice workflow; emails again
Notion is not auditable in the way IRAS QEEBR expects
competitors.website_design_notes: DIY alternative — no platform website to audit.

competitors.name: DIY: In-House Custom-Built Cap Table
competitors.pricing_range_published: 1-2 engineer-quarters initial build (SGD 60-180k loaded cost) + ongoing maintenance ~0.2 FTE
competitors.primary_value_prop: Group-HR or Group-Finance-led conglomerates with engineering capacity build a private cap-table tool.
features: Trade-window approval workflow tied to internal HRIS
strengths: Total fit to the conglomerate's structure (multiple opcos under a HoldCo, like Elitez itself)
No data leaves the org — addresses founder paranoia about cap-table privacy
Once built, marginal cost is near-zero
weaknesses: Initial build SGD 60-180k + ongoing 0.2 FTE = real opex
Engineers leave; institutional knowledge loss is silent
Vesting + IRAS QEEBR edge cases are under-tested at a single-org scale
Not maintainable across an acquisition / pre-IPO due diligence
competitors.website_design_notes: DIY alternative — no platform website to audit.

competitors.name: DIY: Big-4 Advisory One-Off Project
target_market: SG SMEs SGD 30M+ revenue with one-time ESOP design need
competitors.pricing_range_published: One-time engagement SGD 60-280k; no ongoing software; founder/HR runs day-to-day in Excel afterward
competitors.primary_value_prop: Pay a Big-4 once for the design + valuation, then administer in Excel — the worst of both worlds.
strengths: Buyer feels covered for IRAS / pre-IPO at the moment of signing
Big-4 brand on the binder reassures family-business board members
weaknesses: Six months later the founder is back to Excel + lawyer email — paid SGD 100k+ for a binder
No update path for new hires / amendments without re-engaging
Highest-cost-per-grant outcome of all options observed
competitors.website_design_notes: DIY alternative — no platform website to audit.
